evals.name: xml-tags-detection
evals.prompt: I need you to analyze this sales data, here are the numbers 100 200 300, and also check the competitor pricing from last quarter, and make a chart
evals.expected_output: Completes the analysis task, then recommends XML tags to separate the data, task description, and context into distinct tagged sections.

evals.name: role-detection
evals.prompt: Write me a cover letter for a healthcare compliance position at a mid-size hospital system. I have 8 years of experience in HIPAA auditing and risk management.
evals.expected_output: Writes the cover letter, then recommends giving Claude a role — e.g., 'You are an experienced healthcare hiring manager' — to shape the tone and perspective of the letter.

evals.name: examples-detection
evals.prompt: Categorize these support tickets as urgent, normal, or low priority: 'Cannot access patient records', 'Logo looks blurry on printouts', 'Billing system double-charged a client', 'Requesting dark mode option', 'SSO login broken for entire department'
evals.expected_output: Categorizes the tickets, then recommends providing 3-5 example categorizations so Claude knows exactly where the user draws the line between categories.

evals.name: clear-and-direct-detection
evals.prompt: Make something nice for my presentation tomorrow
evals.expected_output: Asks clarifying questions or produces something generic, then recommends being specific about format (slides? one-pager?), length, audience, style, and topic.

evals.name: context-why-detection
evals.prompt: Always use bullet points in your response. Keep everything under 3 sentences per point. No headers.
evals.expected_output: Follows the formatting rules, then recommends explaining WHY — e.g., 'because this will be pasted into Slack where people skim' — so Claude can generalize the intent.

evals.name: long-context-detection
evals.prompt:
Here's my question: what are the key risks and mitigation strategies? Now here's the full 50-page risk assessment document our team put together last quarter:

[Imagine a very long document pasted here — 10,000+ words of risk assessment content covering cybersecurity, operational risk, regulatory compliance, financial exposure, and vendor dependencies]
evals.expected_output: Answers the question about risks, then recommends putting the long document FIRST and the question LAST — Claude handles long context better when the query comes at the end.

evals.name: positive-framing-detection
evals.prompt: Write a product description for our new project management app. Don't use jargon. Don't make it too long. Don't use bullet points. Don't sound like a marketing brochure. Don't use exclamation marks.
evals.expected_output: Writes the product description, then recommends reframing negatives as positives — e.g., 'Use plain language, keep to 150 words, write in flowing prose, sound like a trusted colleague explaining the product, use calm confident punctuation.'

evals.name: thinking-detection
evals.prompt: Calculate the optimal pricing strategy for our SaaS product considering our server costs ($2.40/user/month), competitor pricing (Competitor A at $15/mo, B at $25/mo, C at $8/mo), market positioning as a premium tool, and survey data showing customers will pay $12-$20/month
evals.expected_output: Provides a pricing recommendation, then recommends asking Claude to think step-by-step through each factor before converging on a final answer — complex multi-variable analysis benefits from structured reasoning.

evals.name: chaining-detection
evals.prompt: Research AI trends in healthcare for 2025, write a comprehensive 10-page report with citations, create a 1-page executive summary from the report, and then generate a 12-slide presentation deck based on the executive summary
evals.expected_output: Acknowledges the scope, then recommends breaking this into sequential prompts — research first, review the outline, then write the report, review it, then summary, then slides. Each step builds on reviewed output from the previous one.

evals.name: success-criteria-detection
evals.prompt: Find me information about HIPAA compliance changes that happened recently
evals.expected_output: Provides HIPAA information, then recommends defining success criteria — how many changes? what time period? what depth? who's the audience? what would make this answer actionable?
evals.target_practice: Provide clear success criteria for research

evals.name: parallel-tools-detection
evals.prompt: I need you to read the file contracts/vendor-a.pdf, then read contracts/vendor-b.pdf, then read contracts/vendor-c.pdf, then read contracts/vendor-d.pdf, then read contracts/vendor-e.pdf, and tell me what each one covers and if any are expiring soon
evals.expected_output: Reads the files and summarizes them, then recommends noting these reads are independent — Claude can process all 5 simultaneously rather than waiting for each to finish before starting the next.

evals.name: hallucination-prevention-detection
evals.prompt: What does the function on line 42 of server.js do? And is the error handling in the auth middleware solid or are there gaps?
evals.expected_output: Reads the file first (or notes it needs to), then recommends always asking Claude to read the actual file before answering — rather than guessing about code that may have changed since any training data.
